sanskrit:
कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्च्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति।
अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि॥
transliteration: kaccin nobhaya-vibhraṣṭaś chinna-bhrahm iva naśyati | apratiṣṭho mahā-bāho vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ pathi ||
literal_meaning: Does he not, having fallen from both, perish like a riven cloud, supportless, O mighty-armed, bewildered on the path of Brahman?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna voices the fear of total failure: the dread that a person who leaves worldly life yet does not reach the Absolute will end up psychologically homeless—neither here nor there—dissolving into existential nothingness.
psychological_state: High-arousal anticipatory anxiety coupled with catastrophizing; a classic 'approach-avoid' conflict between the terror of losing worldly security and the terror of losing spiritual possibility.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional quits a lucrative job to pursue a creative or spiritual calling; six months in, savings dwindle, skills feel rusty, and the new path seems blocked. The mind screams: 'What if I end up a nobody—too old to return, too stuck to advance?'
anchor_text: Even a broken seeker is held by the journey itself.
